summary: NotaryChain Verify (public document integrity + Trust Badge revenue stream) tested end-to-end. 20/20 backend pytest passed. Frontend /verify public page, badge banner, all 3 tabs, hash verification, /badges protected page, Create/Embed/Verify-Domain modals all functional.

minor.issue: Returns 'document_hash' field instead of 'sha256' as stated in review spec. Frontend uses 'document_hash' so functionality fine, but spec naming mismatch.
Uses await db.list_collection_names() inside endpoint on every call to detect 'fraud_flags' — extra round-trip per request. Could cache or wrap in try/except.
DNS TXT lookup uses synchronous dns.resolver.resolve() inside an async endpoint — blocks event loop. Recommend using dns.asyncresolver or running in thread pool.

issues: React console warning: 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop' from Dashboard render method (pre-existing, not from /verify or /badges)

action_items: (Optional/spec) Add 'sha256' alias key in POST /api/verify/document response to match documented contract — frontend already reads document_hash so no urgent fix.
(Perf) Replace sync dns.resolver.resolve in verify-domain endpoint with async resolver or run_in_executor to avoid blocking the event loop.
(Cleanup) Cache db.list_collection_names() result instead of calling per request in verify_notary_public_profile.
(Pre-existing, not in scope) Fix Dashboard list missing key prop React warning.
critical_code_review_comments: verify_routes.py is well structured and within size limits (~400 lines). _seal_response helper is good DRY.
MongoDB _id correctly excluded from all responses via projection {'_id': 0}.
Public endpoints correctly skip auth; protected endpoints use _get_user with bearer token.
/badges/{id}/verify-domain correctly returns 200 with structured instructions on failure (NOT raising 4xx) — matches spec.
Badge JSON endpoint correctly excludes verification_token, user_id, user_email — verified by test.
widget.js correctly substitutes PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL — verified content includes the public backend URL and /api/verify/badge/ path.
SVG impressions counter increments on every public render — verified.
Free-tier user blocked with structured 403 + upgrade_required (contains 'pro' and pricing) — verified.
Owner-only authorization enforced on delete and verify-domain — verified with demo user.
Synchronous DNS resolver call inside async endpoint is a perf concern under load.
test_credentials: admin@notarychain.com / Admin123! (used for protected flows). demo@test.com / Demo123! used for free-tier 403 verification.
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST_acme-test.com and TEST_deleteme.com badges for admin during test run. Existing badge 2d38708fb6fa44a0 (Acme Test Inc.) preserved and used for /verify?badge banner test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_verify_trust_badges.py covers all 11 verify endpoints + regressions. Run with: export $(grep -v '^#' /app/frontend/.env | xargs) && pytest /app/backend/tests/test_verify_trust_badges.py -v. /verify is public and /verify?badge=<id> shows banner. /badges requires login (admin or paid user). DNS TXT verification for acme-test.com WILL return verified=false with instructions — this is expected and tested.
verified_flows: GET /api/verify/document/{hash} — invalid hash 400, valid 64-char unknown returns verified=false
POST /api/verify/document — multipart upload returns verified=false with document_hash
POST /api/verify/badges — free user 403 upgrade_required, admin 200 success
GET /api/verify/badges — returns owner's badges only, 401 without auth
DELETE /api/verify/badges/{id} — owner can delete, other user 403
POST /api/verify/badges/{id}/verify-domain — returns 200 verified=false + instructions (dns_txt + well_known) when DNS not set; non-owner 403
/verify page — public, all 3 tabs functional, hash verify shows Not Found result
/verify?badge=2d38708fb6fa44a0 — green badge banner with business name/domain/status visible
/badges page — protected, shows existing badge card with Verify/Embed/Delete buttons
Verify Domain modal — auto-runs verify, displays DNS TXT (host/type/value) and well-known (URL/content) instructions
